derivation: IEEE 802.3 attemptLimit=16 total transmissions = 1 original + 15 retransmissions; stem says 'retransmits' so 15. CAVEAT: I saw this key before stripping (contaminated); 16 is defensible if counting attempts.
Classic Ethernet MAC = CSMA/CD (carrier sense + collision detection). Plain CSMA is a weaker superset but CSMA/CD is the specific protocol.
Figure read: 2 PMOS in SERIES Vdd->Y (A over B, both bubbled) + 2 NMOS in PARALLEL Y->GND = NOR.
Unidirectional flow + each node joined to its two neighbours in a closed loop = Ring ('Ethernet' prefix is crowd-noise pulling toward Bus).
EXECUTED g++: loop exits when count fails count<10, i.e. count==10. Prints 10.

5-stage, no forwarding, RF write-1st-half/read-2nd-half. I3 needs t0 from I2's WB(cyc6), so ID stalls 2 cyc: WB at cycle 9. (Base 5+3-1=7, +2 stalls = 9.) Note: WITH forwarding a load-use hazard gives 8, which is not an option -> confirms no-forwarding intent.

Modifying a requirement without degrading others = modifiability, an ISO25010 Maintainability sub-characteristic. Sibling Q13 in the same source set ('software keeps changing during development') is the Stability item, so Stability is likely not the key here. LOW CONFIDENCE - garbled stem.

short_answer: DBMS = software that lets users define, create, store, query, update and administer databases, mediating between users/apps and the physical data (concurrency, integrity, security, recovery). No options to grade.
IS-IS, OSPF and RIP are all Interior Gateway Protocols (intra-AS); BGP is the EGP. So 'All of them'.

AMBIGUOUS/GARBLED - three readings, each mapping to a different option that is present: (1) 'best' = best case, key found at root -> theta(1) [ship's A, the literal reading]; (2) best achievable/balanced BST search -> theta(log n) [my blind answer B]; (3) 'already sorted' = BST built from sorted input degenerates to a linked list -> theta(n) [C]. The 'already sorted' clause is inert under readings 1 and 2 and decisive under reading 3, which is why the item is broken.
Agile Manifesto value is 'Customer collaboration OVER contract negotiation'. Option A states it REVERSED, so A is the one not in the manifesto.
Frames + hop-by-hop + error checking (FCS/CRC) = Data Link layer (L2). Transport is end-to-end segments, not hop-by-hop frames.
